solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int calculate(String s) {
        int num = 0;
        int result = 0;
        char operation = '+';
        Stack<Integer> stack = new Stack<>();

        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            char c = s.charAt(i);
            if (Character.isDigit(c)) {
                num = num * 10 + (c - '0');
            } else if (c != ' ') {
                processOperation(num, operation, stack);
                num = 0;
                operation = c;
                if (c == '(') {
                    stack.push(result);
                    stack.push(operation);
                    result = 0;
                }
            }
        }
        processOperation(num, operation, stack);

        return result;
    }

    private void processOperation(int num, char operation, Stack<Integer> stack) {
        if (operation == '+') {
            stack.push(num);
        } else if (operation == '-') {
            stack.push(-num);
        } else if (operation == '*') {
            stack.push(stack.pop() * num);
        } else if (operation == '/') {
            stack.push(stack.pop() / num);
        }
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a stack to handle the order of operations and supports parentheses. It processes each character in the string once, maintaining a running result and using the stack to manage intermediate values and operations. The key strength is its linear time complexity and efficient use of the stack to manage operations. The weakness is the use of extra space for the stack, which is proportional to the input size.>
